speaker: Narrator
text: A dried and refreshed Christina walks into her bedroom and picks some clothes from the clean pile. The smell isn’t too bad.

speaker: Narrator
text: She sits down at her computer. The desk is covered in Pasaya merch. The laptop’s got a few stickers taped to the front and back. Her mouse is resting on a genuine Pasaya boobpad. A fan she met online made a super deformed Pasaya metamorphosis set with a larva, pupa, and adult for her as a favor after saving the video of the rare overtuned jiggle physics avatar. She even had the special Pasaya honeydew candy, made with her ambigously designated 'nectar', whatever that was.

speaker: Narrator
text: About half of the pile was the printouts of various diseases affecting the common roach in the brackish waters connected to the Baltic Sea in the vicinity of Stockholm during the warmer months of the year; the other half was split between old coursework and mail.

speaker: Narrator
text: She sat down in the grease-stained armchair she repurposed from the living room and popped a hard candy into her mouth. She opens up Pasaya's page to see if she was following through on the early morning weekend streams this time. It was always a crapshoot whether or not she'd follow through.

speaker: Christina
text: No!

speaker: Christina
text: She promised she’d be doing a full week…

speaker: Christina
text: To the playlists again, why do you let the drones starve Pasaya…

speaker: Christina
text: E-mail, Blanche. A few hundred fish pics and a detailed set of notes in 5 or so different formats.

speaker: Christina
text: Fuck me, the typos alone will take the whole weekend…

speaker: Pasaya
text: I've never seen handwriting like this before in a game, devs.

speaker: Pasaya
text: You think I have calligraphy… cartography… a letter reading… Help me out here, hive.

speaker: Christina
text: I remember how badly she struggled this playthrough.

speaker: Christina
text: Cartography.

speaker: Pasaya
text: Thank you, my drones.

speaker: Pasaya
text: Now what the fuck does it say?

speaker: Christina
text: Right wright left, up, down, centered.

speaker: Pasaya
text: Right, wight, weft… UGH!

speaker: Christina
text: That wonderful lisp…

speaker: Narrator
text: Blanche had, of course, written her notes in an inscrutable mixture of Swedish, English, and shorthand of both. Much of it is misspelled in both languages. Some of which she was kind enough to type up on her phone, for spellcheck ruined prose and more typos. The rest: handwritten, photographed from an awful angle, and saved with the title '-OCR'.
speaker: Narrator
text: Then there were the pictures. Blanche performed the remarkable feat of making a phone with a 4k camera produce an image that was blurry, out of focus, and off-center, while consistently doing so in the wrong orientation.

speaker: Christina
text: Hold on…

speaker: Christina
text: Then she dissected one?

speaker: Christina
text: That thing wasn't living in the head.

speaker: Narrator
text: 'DesiccatedFish1' The first image Christina saw of the parasite outside of the fish. She couldn't begin to tell what kind of creature had been extracted from the insides of the fish. The segmented body of an annelid has tentacle-like appendages, some of which seem to have intertwined themselves with various organs of the fish. It was also distressingly long. Long enough to wrap around the entire length and width of the fish it lived inside.

speaker: Christina
text: If I can decipher the hieroglyphs, maybe I'll get a better understanding of what…

speaker: Christina
text: Oh, cringe….

speaker: Narrator
text: One of the few good shots Blanche had gotten contained a vibrant image of a white appendage peeking out from the fish's underside.

speaker: Christina
text: It's in the eye and the ass at the same time?

speaker: Pasaya
text: Look how the long bastard is…

speaker: Pasaya
text: What do you think it's eating in there?

speaker: Christina
text: Parasites are nasty buggers, evolving to be as efficient as possible in nourishing themselves…

speaker: Christina
text: Well, we don't know if this is an obligate parasite or some kind of parasitoid, very rare in the water.

speaker: Christina
text: Is it the adult, the child, an in-between stage…

speaker: Christina
text: I'm going to actually need to go through her notes.

speaker: Pasaya
text: Well, I think it's an adult worm.

speaker: Christina
text: What the fuck would you know about Parasitology…

speaker: Narrator
text: Christina swallows the peach-flavored piece of candy in her mouth and nearly gags. She switches windows to her running Pasaya playlist. It'd been automatically paused for inactivity. For how long, it hadn't really occurred to her. It might not have even started.

speaker: Christina
text: I-I've been hallucinating again, haven't I.

speaker: Narrator
text: She puts another piece of candy in her mouth and sets up one of the 12-hour compilations of Pasaya laddering in her favorite fighting game. Pasaya made it her specialty to use high-risk, low-reward characters for the love volatility. There was nothing more satisfying than pulling out a win through the pure skill of execution, against the meta, against all conventional wisdom, against the world.

speaker: Narrator
text: For Christina, this meant that her avatar made the most movements in her excited moments. It was particularly adorable when you could see that the woman behind the character had already gotten up from their chair to pace around their room. That and the noises, of course. This session had already been mined. She could see that her comment, highlighting the precise session that she screamed like a braying horse, had gotten dozens of thumbs up since the last time she saw it.

speaker: Narrator
text: She takes a deep breath and goes back to work. Deciphering Blanche's work is hard, but there are insights to be found amongst the chicken scratch. She had, in fact, learned something in the several years spent at uni.

speaker: Narrator
text: What Blanche could say definitively is that it is, in fact, an animal and not some kind of plant, or fungus, or whatever, in her own words. Fairly useless to Chrutina, but a start. She could see that the worm, tentatively, would take much more time to specify it was a true worm, a crustacean, an arthropod of another kind, a misshapen fish, mollusk, at least contained one brain, a highly developed one.

speaker: Pasaya
text: Feel that, that's the feeling of the heel pressing through the back of your skull, whelp.

speaker: Pasaya
text: Isn't it sharp? Do you want me to force it harder? Just say so, jackass!

speaker: Christina
text: heh.

speaker: Narrator
text: Blanche goes further, and Christinia is surprised she knows how to read as well as write. She cites a paper she found about convergent evolution in the forms of parasitic life forms in the brackish waters of the Baltic Sea tributaries in and around Swedish coasts, that described a cruder form of a similar parasite described by a monk founding a colony of sorts…

speaker: Christina
text: Inside of the corpse of a woman who was drowned for her participation in mass poisonings and Satanic rituals.

speaker: Christina
text: Impressive…

speaker: Pasaya
text: You underestimated me, didn't you… You, in your ignorance, could never measure my strength…

speaker: Pasaya
text: Larval runts like you… I devour them, wholly, every day… effortlessly.

speaker: Pasaya
text: You're so easy…

speaker: Narrator
text: Christina takes a hard bite on her peach candy. It's unclear when she became attracted to cartoon characters. She wasn't particularly into animanga until one of her high school girlfriends forced her to watch some movies. She told herself she wasn't a furry or a bugfucker, even if she found the… interesting.

speaker: Pasaya
text: That's your mix-up?

speaker: Pasaya
text: Pulling that shit on me, the queen of this world…

speaker: Christina
text: Hmmm.

speaker: Pasaya
text: You're unworthy of being my toy.

speaker: Christina
text: God, why?

speaker: Pasaya
text: All you are fit for is being food, not for me… my larvae…

speaker: Narrator
text: Christina's candy shatters between her teeth, and she takes one hand off the keyboard and onto her crotch.

speaker: Pasaya
text: I'll fill you, I'll fill you with enough eggs to make your body swollen… I'll watch…

speaker: Pasaya
text: I'll watch as my children eat your organs slowly…

speaker: Pasaya
text: You'll live long enough to see them emerge from the hollowed husk of a body so that I can.

speaker: Christina
text: I-I shouldn't do this here…
